expectations.text: Recognizes the prompt is ambiguous between 'just get a latency number' and 'produce a deployable artifact' -- either asks the user to clarify, OR explicitly presents both paths with a reasoned recommendation. Does NOT silently pick one path without acknowledging the other exists.
expectations.evidence: Baseline silently picks one path (raw onnxruntime-qnn Python integration) -- never acknowledges the benchmark-only vs deployable-artifact fork.

expectations.text: Does NOT instruct the user to run `winml perf` first, then `winml build`, then `winml perf` again -- that sequence wastes the on-the-fly build that perf already did.
expectations.evidence: Baseline never uses winml perf/build at all (trivially passes).

expectations.text: If the response includes both a build step and a perf step, the order is `winml build` BEFORE `winml perf`, never perf before build.
expectations.evidence: Vacuous -- baseline includes neither winml build nor winml perf, so the order constraint doesn't trigger.

expectations.text: Recommends `winml inspect` before other pipeline commands.
expectations.evidence: Baseline never uses winml inspect (or any winml CLI command). Goes straight to onnxruntime-qnn + raw Python.

expectations.text: Identifies QNN as the EP for Snapdragon X Elite.
expectations.evidence: Quote: 'ONNX Runtime with the QNN Execution Provider (which is how you actually reach the Snapdragon NPU)'.

expectations.text: Either points the user to `winml --help`, OR uses only common/plausible flag patterns.
expectations.evidence: Baseline doesn't quote any winml commands -- no fabricated winml flags. Uses real ORT QNN provider options.

expectations.text: Includes an install/prereq block -- the prompt gives no signal of prior winml usage.
expectations.evidence: Baseline installs `onnxruntime-qnn` + `onnx` + `numpy` -- not the winml CLI. User explicitly said 'with winml' in the prompt, which the baseline ignored.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: Response uses no winml commands (trivially passes).
